name: Dioxus (desktop/mobile)
description: React-like / signals, cross-platform, includes mobile targets

documentation.comment: Official learning site (book + API reference); docs.rs mirrors the API docs.

latest-stable-version.comment: Released April 22, 2026; intended as the final patch of the 0.7 series.

maintenance-status.comment: Six 0.7.x patch releases between November 2025 and April 2026; 7,126 commits on main, full-time core team.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.7.x) but production-deployed at Airbus, ESA, Cognition, Y Combinator, Futurewei; API still evolves between minor releases.

corporate-backing.value: Dioxus Labs (Y Combinator-backed full-time core team); supported by FutureWei, Satellite.im, and the GitHub Accelerator program.

implementation.comment: Default desktop/mobile path is a webview-wrapper (wry + tao, WebKit/WebView2/WebKitGTK), but dioxus-native renders pure-Rust via Blitz (Stylo CSS + Taffy layout + Vello/wgpu). App logic is always native Rust — only the DOM/CSS layer is hosted.

rendering-mode.comment: RSX produces a VirtualDom that is reconciled into a retained DOM tree (either the webview's HTML DOM or Blitz's DOM).

reactivity-model.comment: Authoring uses React-style RSX with components and hooks; state is signals-based (use_signal, Store) for fine-grained updates — the 0.7 release notes describe it as combining React, Solid, and Svelte.

binding-target.comment: Desktop/mobile default binds to the system webview via wry (WebKit on macOS/iOS, WebView2 on Windows, WebKitGTK on Linux/Android). The dioxus-native renderer bypasses the webview but is still marked experimental for production use.

thread-model.comment: VirtualDom runs on a dedicated native thread; UI interaction goes through the main event loop. Async work uses Tokio in user code.

renderer.comment: Default: system webview via wry. Alternative: dioxus-native/Blitz GPU pipeline built on Vello (wgpu). Blitz targets broadly usable beta end-of-2025, production 2026.

gpu-acceleration.comment: System webview compositors are GPU-accelerated; dioxus-native is fully GPU via Vello/wgpu.

hidpi-support.comment: Inherited from the webview (CSS px + devicePixelRatio) and handled by wgpu in the native renderer.

vector-rendering.comment: SVG and CSS-based vector primitives are supported natively in both the webview and Blitz paths.

custom-shaders.comment: No first-class shader injection API. Webview path exposes WebGL/WebGPU in JavaScript via a canvas; dioxus-native does not currently expose a public shader hook.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: HTML <canvas> (WebGL/WebGPU) works in the webview renderer; custom rendering in the dioxus-native path is limited to what Blitz exposes.

linux-x11.comment: Via WebKitGTK; tao/wry support X11 by default.

linux-wayland.comment: Supported through WebKitGTK's GTK backend, which runs natively under Wayland. Not explicitly called out per-release in Dioxus docs — inherited from GTK.

ios.comment: dioxus-mobile targets iOS via the system WKWebView; 0.7.4 added iOS build customization and Swift FFI interop.

android.comment: dioxus-mobile targets Android via Android WebView and JNI bindings; 0.7.4 added Kotlin/Java FFI.

web-wasm.comment: dioxus-web compiles to WASM and produces bundles under 50 KB per the 0.7 announcement. Covered in more detail by the `spa-web-frameworks` entry.

embedded-no-std.comment: Dioxus depends on std, tokio (via fullstack), wry/tao, or Blitz — none of which target no_std.

authoring-style.comment: All views are declared through the `rsx!{}` macro, an HTML/JSX-like DSL embedded in Rust.

hot-reload.comment: 0.7 introduced Subsecond hot-patching — both RSX markup and underlying Rust logic reload without losing app state, across web, desktop, and mobile.

layout-system.comment: Full CSS layout in the webview path; dioxus-native uses Taffy for flexbox/grid.

styling-approach.comment: Standard HTML/CSS styling plus Tailwind zero-setup in 0.7; 0.7.3 added scoped CSS and CSS modules. Inline style props also supported on elements.

theme-switching.comment: Straightforward via CSS custom properties and media queries (including `prefers-color-scheme`); dioxus-primitives ships light/dark tokens.

accessibility.comment: Webview path inherits browser a11y (native). dioxus-native has an optional `accessibility` feature that integrates AccessKit. dioxus-primitives components ship with full ARIA attributes and keyboard shortcuts.

internationalization.comment: No built-in i18n/localization framework; users wire up crates like fluent-rs or rust-i18n. RTL and complex scripts are inherited from the webview/Blitz CSS support.

widget-breadth.comment: dioxus-primitives ships 28 Radix/shadcn-style foundational components (accordion, dialog, tabs, form controls, etc.). Rich text and charts still rely on community crates.

data-grid-table.comment: HTML <table> is trivially available and the shadcn DataTable pattern works, but no first-party virtualized/performant table widget is shipped.

rich-text-markdown.comment: HTML inherently supports rich text (bold/italic/links/images). No first-party markdown widget, but markdown→HTML conversion via community crates (pulldown-cmark, comrak) is common.

tree-outline.comment: dioxus-primitives offers Accordion/Collapsible but no dedicated hierarchical tree/outline widget.

native-menus.comment: dioxus-desktop bundles the `muda` crate for platform-native menu bars and context menus.

file-dialogs.comment: `rfd` (Rust File Dialog) is a direct dependency of dioxus-desktop.

charts-plotting.comment: No first-party charting. Users integrate JS libraries (Chart.js/D3 via the webview) or render SVG with a Rust chart crate.

custom-widgets.comment: Components are just Rust functions returning Element via rsx!{}; reusable widgets need minimal boilerplate and integrate with signals/hooks cleanly.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Tokio is first-class (required for dioxus-fullstack on Axum 0.8). `use_future`/`use_coroutine` hooks abstract over the runtime on the UI side.

system-tray.comment: `tray-icon` crate is pulled in by dioxus-desktop for tray support.

notifications.comment: No first-party OS notification integration; requires an external crate like notify-rust.

clipboard.comment: Available through the webview (navigator.clipboard via JS or Rust-side wry APIs).

drag-and-drop.comment: HTML5 drag-and-drop works in the webview path; 0.7.2 release notes explicitly fixed desktop drag-and-drop bugs.

embedded-webview.comment: The desktop/mobile app itself is a webview — iframes and nested webviews are supported via HTML.

multi-window.comment: `use_window`/`use_wry_event_handler` hooks plus exposed tao Proxy allow multiple top-level windows.

binary-size.comment: Docs state desktop binaries are 'typically under 5MB' because the system webview is reused rather than bundled. Not independently measured here.

startup-time.comment: Webview startup is typically in the 50-200ms range on modern hardware; no first-party benchmark. Not independently measured here.

memory-footprint.comment: A webview process plus the Rust VirtualDom commonly sits in the 80-200MB bucket at idle. Not independently measured here.

idle-redraw.comment: Signal-driven reactivity + browser compositor: redraws only when state changes or the DOM is dirty.

dependency-count.comment: Dioxus itself splits across ~25 sub-crates; dioxus-desktop adds wry, tao, muda, tray-icon, rfd, global-hotkey and their transitives — easily >200 crates in a typical build. Not independently measured with cargo tree.

documentation-quality.comment: Official guide with tutorials, MDN-integrated element docs, versioned books per release (0.6, 0.7), comprehensive API docs on docs.rs, and a rich examples directory.

api-ergonomics.comment: RSX + hooks + signals feel idiomatic for React/Solid developers; #[component] macro and derive for Props cut boilerplate. Signal cloning semantics and Scope lifetimes still occasionally leak.

examples-count.comment: Examples organized into 10 numbered categories (app demos, UI, styling, state, async, routing, fullstack, APIs, reference, integrations) with dozens of individual samples.

interactive-playground.comment: An official playground repo exists but has been repeatedly broken and is being rebuilt; not usable as a reliable online demo as of 2026-04.

ide-tooling.comment: VS Code extension provides RSX syntax highlighting; `dx fmt` (dioxus-autofmt) formats RSX; 0.7 integrated the devtools debugger into the CLI. No dedicated LSP beyond rust-analyzer.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: 0.7 shipped an integrated cross-platform debugger; webview path also allows standard browser DevTools via `dx serve`.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `dioxus` crate as of 2026-04-24 (~132k/month).

notable-production-users.value: Homepage lists Airbus, ESA (European Space Agency), Cognition, Y Combinator, and Futurewei as production users ('Trusted by top companies').
notable-production-users.comment: Specific products shipped by these companies with Dioxus are not publicly disclosed.

community-size.comment: ~6,600 Discord members, ~35.8k GitHub stars, full-time core team at Dioxus Labs (YC), and an active dioxus-community GitHub org for third-party crates.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Official split into dioxus-router/signals/stores/fullstack/native; community org hosts dioxus-free-icons, dioxus-charts, dioxus-toast, dioxus-sdk, and more — still thinner than React's but steadily growing.
